Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **4/10** WARNING with 3+ signals active vs Divergence composite zone CALM at **41.86%** diverging pairs
Crash board flags discrete stress points (credit, vol, breadth) while the cross-asset correlation map still looks orderly. Trust the Crash Index here: it catches regime shifts before the correlation tape rebuilds around them.
⚠ These two disagree: VIX Spike Tier **2/4** MEDIUM with backwardation and skew active at **60%** prob vs Zero active confluence triggers (T1 x HYG, T1 x B2, T1 x JPY all inactive)
Options market is pricing a vol expansion in isolation. Credit, small caps and yen haven't joined. Trust the confluence read for sizing: standalone vol stress is a hedge signal, not a tail-event positioning signal.
⚠ These two disagree: Macro regime BROADENING (constructive rotation) vs BROADENING confidence only **30%** plus NDX **-2.5%** on 5d
The regime label says healthy rotation, the confidence and tech bleed say it could be forced selling at the top. Trust the **30%** confidence: lean to the call but do not size it like a high-conviction regime.
Today's tension
Small caps lead while mega-cap tech bleeds, and the crash board lights up under a calm vol surface.
Bull
Rotation is the story. **RUT +1.6%** beating **NDX -2.5%** with macro regime tagged BROADENING is the textbook setup for a healthier market where capital leaves a narrow top and funds the next **400** stocks.
Bear
Crash Index sits at **4/10** WARNING with three plus signals active, VIX backwardation and skew are both on, and BROADENING confidence is only **30%**. Rotations born inside late-cycle stress often end as drawdowns, not new uptrends.
The tell: Whether the Mother Jaws composite stays in CALM at **41.86%** diverging pairs or pushes the **13** bearish count higher. If bearish divergences expand while NDX keeps bleeding, the rotation flips into a broad de-risking.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is a rotation wearing a warning sticker. Crash Index prints 4/10 WARNING with three plus signals active, VIX Spike sits at tier 2/4 MEDIUM at 60% probability, regime state reads 44 TRANSITION with MIXED bias, and the macro regime tag is BROADENING but only at 30% confidence. You have small caps leading (RUT +1.6%) while mega-cap tech bleeds (NDX -2.5%), which is the constructive read, and you have credit and Treasury correlations snapping into new regimes, which is the destructive read. Both can be true at once and today they are.

Position for rotation, hedge for regime change. The cleanest expression is equal-weight or small-cap long versus a cap-weighted tech short, sized modestly because BROADENING confidence is 30% and not 70%. Use the active VIX backwardation and skew as a green light to keep tail hedges on rather than letting them roll off, since the options market is paying up for protection while spot indices stay orderly. Contrarian score at 37 and divergence zone CALM at 41.86% mean you are not yet in a contrarian buy zone, so do not chase the laggards (HSI, NDX) on the assumption that the rotation immediately mean reverts.

The risk is that the rotation is forced rather than chosen. If the 13 bearish divergences expand and the composite leaves CALM, the small-cap bid was funded by tech selling, and the next leg is broad de-risking, not broadening. The reading that flips the stance: composite divergence zone moving out of CALM with bearish pairs climbing past 20 of 43, or Crash Index pushing to 6/10. Either takes you from lean-long-broadening to flat with hedges paid.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is the label you want at the strength you do not. Broadening means leadership is widening from the mega-cap top into the rest of the market, and the RUT +1.6% versus NDX -2.5% 5-day spread is the cleanest evidence.

30% confidence means the model sees the rotation but cannot yet rule out that it is forced selling masquerading as healthy breadth. Position the call directionally (equal-weight over cap-weighted, small caps over Nasdaq megas), size it at half what you would run in a high-confidence regime, and add only if confidence climbs and the divergence composite stays in CALM.

If NDX bleed accelerates while small caps stall, the regime tag will downgrade and you cut.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The composite sits in CALM zone at 41.86% of pairs diverging, with 13 bearish and 5 bullish out of 43 pairs and net bias +8, while Mother Jaws score reads 100. Plain English: a divergence pair is two assets that usually move in a known relationship; correlation drift is how that relationship has shifted on rolling windows.

When drift flips sign, the pair is sending a regime-change signal. Today the heavy hitters are stlfsi4_d20 at score 71 DEEP DIVERG with drift from +0.37 to -0.05 (financial stress has decoupled from its prior driver), lqd_ief jumping from -0.09 to +0.86 (investment grade credit now tracks Treasuries tightly, a flight to quality footprint), t10y2y flipping +0.64 to -0.56 (the 2s10s curve has changed character), and spy_rsp moving -0.04 to +0.88 (cap-weighted SPY now moves with equal-weight RSP, which is the breadth catch-up that the BROADENING tag is built on).

The mix is constructive on breadth, defensive on credit. Watch the bearish count: if it walks from 13 toward 20, you leave CALM and the breadth read dies first.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of the three confluence triggers are active. VIX backwardation has not paired with HYG credit stress, with small-cap drawdown (B2), or with yen safe-haven flow.

That matters for how you lean. Standalone vol stress is a signal to keep hedges on, but absent credit or yen confirmation it is not a signal to flatten risk or chase puts.

Stay long the rotation (small caps over mega-cap tech), keep tail hedges paid through the VIX backwardation window, and treat the first confluence print (especially T1 x HYG, since credit leads equities into stress) as the moment to cut gross. Until one lights up, the vol bid is a hedging signal, not a positioning signal.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

Three of the four 24/7 tapes are split, and one is bleeding. Asia: N225 +0.6% leading, HSI -6.1% lagging, which is Japan over China and a clean liquidity-out-of-China read.

Europe: SSMI +1.1% over FCHI -1.3%, defensive Swiss over cyclical France, so European internals are tilting risk-off. US: RUT +1.6% over NDX -2.5%, the rotation that drives the BROADENING macro tag.

Crypto: SOL -2.8% is the leader and ETH -5.6% the laggard, with both down, which is unambiguous risk-off in the asset class that prints around the clock. The handoff into the US session: defensive Europe and bleeding crypto argue for a soft open, but the small-cap bid in the US tape has held through it.

Lean to a continuation of the RUT over NDX trade intraday, and treat a break in the small-cap bid as the tell that the global risk-off has finally crossed the Atlantic.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 4/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 4/10 WARNING and VIX Spike at tier 2/4 MEDIUM look mismatched on the surface, but they describe the same market from two angles. The Crash board counts discrete stress signals firing across credit, breadth and divergence, and three plus are on.

The VIX detector reads the options market, where backwardation and skew are active (front-month vol bid over back-month, downside puts bid over upside calls) at 60% probability of a spike, while the adaptive flag has not triggered. Translation: spot indices are still orderly, so realized vol stays low, but dealers and hedgers are paying up for near-term protection because the underlying signals are stacking.

Coherent state, not a contradiction. Keep tail hedges funded while you stay long the rotation, because the options market is telling you the next vol print is more likely up than down.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 4/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.

4/10

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (1)
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
